intro.speaker: npc
intro.text: A 'coffee chat' is just a short, informal conversation you have with someone in the industry — to learn about their job and get on their radar. No coffee required. I'll play the banker; you practice answering the kinds of questions we actually ask. No pressure — this one's on me.

q1.speaker: npc
q1.text: So — why are you interested in banking?

choices.label: I'm drawn to the deal flow and the chance to work on high-impact transactions.
choices.feedback: Strong answer. Specific and professional — that's what we want to hear.

choices.label: I want to learn how companies raise capital and do M&A.
choices.feedback: Good direction. In the room, add one concrete example — a deal or skill you want to build.

choices.label: I just want to make a lot of money.
choices.feedback: Be honest with yourself, but in the room lead with intellectual curiosity and the work — not compensation. You'll stand out.

q1_good.speaker: npc
q1_good.text: Good — that's the kind of thing we want to hear. Alright, one more: what's one thing you've done that shows you can handle pressure?

q1_ok.speaker: npc
q1_ok.text: Got it. Next: what's one thing you've done that shows you can handle pressure?

q1_weak.speaker: npc
q1_weak.text: No judgment — just try to focus on the work and the learning curve when you're in the room. What's one thing you've done that shows you can handle pressure?

q2.speaker: npc
q2.text: Give me a quick example — 30 seconds.

choices.label: I led a team project with a tight deadline and we delivered on time.
choices.feedback: Clear and concise. That's the format you want in real chats.

choices.label: I balanced a heavy course load with a part-time role and still hit my grades.
choices.feedback: Solid. In the room, add the outcome — what did you deliver or learn?

choices.label: Um, I've had a lot of deadlines in school...
choices.feedback: Prep one concrete STAR story before your next chat. It makes a huge difference — I'll help you build that here.

q2_good.speaker: npc
q2_good.text: Nice. Last one: how do you stay on top of markets and current events?

q2_weak.speaker: npc
q2_weak.text: You'll get there. One more: how do you stay on top of markets and current events?

q3.speaker: npc
q3.text: We want to see that you're curious — not that you know everything.

choices.label: I read the Wall Street Journal and follow a few deal newsletters; I also skim earnings summaries for companies I'm interested in.
choices.feedback: Specific and believable. That's the bar.

choices.label: I try to check the news and Bloomberg when I can.
choices.feedback: Good start. Name one or two sources you actually use — it sounds more genuine.

choices.label: I don't really follow markets yet, but I'm willing to learn.
choices.feedback: Honest. Before your next chat, pick one deal or headline and have a view. Shows initiative.

end.speaker: npc
end.text: That's the basics. Before we get you in front of anyone else, you need to know your technicals — accounting, valuation, the stuff that comes up in every interview. I'll give you homework: short drills, 10 minutes a day. First coffee chat is on me — you're in.
end.feedback: You've completed your first coffee chat. Head to the map and try a skill drill when you're ready.
